data.author: Muon
data.message:
[python] Support opening MCAP writers on file-like objects (#798)

### Changelog
* Python: extend `open_mcap()` to also accept file-like objects. 

### Docs
None

### Description

This PR extends `open_mcap()` to accept file-like objects in addition to
file paths, enabling MCAP recording to any Python object implementing
`write()`, `seek()`, and `flush()`. This allows using in-memory buffers,
network streams, or similar as MCAP writing sinks.

Closes: FIRE-248

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds file-like object support for MCAP writing in Python.
> 
> - Extends `open_mcap(path, ...)` to accept `str | Path | BinaryIO`;
updates stubs and docstrings in
`python/foxglove/_foxglove_py/__init__.pyi`
> - Implements `PyFileLikeWriter` and `WriterInner` in Rust
(`src/mcap.rs`) to adapt Python objects with `write/seek/flush` to
`Write + Seek`; updates `PyMcapWriter` to use `BufWriter<WriterInner>`
> - Updates `open_mcap` in `src/lib.rs` to handle either filesystem
paths (with `allow_overwrite`) or file-like objects (ignoring overwrite)
> - Adds tests in `python/foxglove/tests/test_mcap.py` for writing to
`BytesIO`, validating MCAP magic bytes, and reading back via
`mcap.reader`
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
1e7f13b31cef6989b20283fbf3e2a4499d9898cc. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

data.author: eloff
data.message:
Core implement ws server listener interface for subs and adverts (#189)

Add new callbacks to ServerListener:

```
fn on_subscribe(&self, _channel_id: ChannelId) {}
fn on_unsubscribe(&self, _channel_id: ChannelId) {}
fn on_client_advertise(&self, _channel: &ClientChannel) {}
fn on_client_unadvertise(&self, _channel_id: ClientChannelId) {}
```

This mirrors how the callbacks are defined in the [Python
implementation](https://github.com/foxglove/ws-protocol/blob/main/python/src/foxglove_websocket/server/__init__.py#L508),
and they function in a similar way (e.g. not firing for duplicate or
erroneous requests)

I added RecordingServerListener to testutil to allow more easily testing
ServerListener.

I modified existing tests to verify these callbacks are called at the
appropriate times with the expected arguments, and not called for
duplicate requests.

data.author: dave-foxglove
data.message:
Rename schemas module to messages in Rust SDK (#893)

## Description
- Renames `foxglove::schemas` to `foxglove::messages` throughout the
Rust SDK
- Adds deprecated `foxglove::schemas` re-export for backward
compatibility
- Updates codegen templates (`generateSdkRustCTypes.ts`,
`foxglove_proto_gen`) to emit `messages` paths
- Updates all internal imports, examples, and C FFI bindings

The `schemas` module contained message type definitions (e.g., `Log`,
`CompressedImage`), not schema format descriptors. Renaming to
`messages` aligns the module name with its contents. The `Schema` struct
and `get_schema()` methods are unchanged.

~44 of the 91 files are pure renames (binary `.bin` data files,
`schemas_wkt` directories) with no content changes. The remaining ~25
are one-line import swaps.

## Changelog

Adding "messages" as an alias of "schemas" in the Rust API with the long
term goal of removing the "schemas" label.

## Docs

Follow-up:
https://linear.app/foxglove/issue/FLE-236/update-docs-to-rename-foxglove-schemas-to-messages

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

data.author: gasmith
data.message:
SDK docs (#172)

I made only small changes to existing wording, because it was well done.
I added docs for types missing docs, hid types that are public for
technical reasons, and renamed TypedMessage trait to Encode after
discussing with Greg.

This doesn't fix the problem of having no readme. I'm not sure if we
should repeat the crate docs, or just part of them, or put something
else there.

---------

data.author: foxglovebot
data.message: Release SDK version v0.1.2

data.author: indirectlylit
data.message:
introduce optional fields and mark LocationFix 'color' as optional (#865)

### Changelog

Make `color` field optional in `LocationFix` messages

### Docs

updated in PR

### Description

In https://github.com/foxglove/foxglove-sdk/pull/556 we added a new
`color` field to `LocationFix` messages. However, introducing this meant
that users could not produce messages which rely on Foxglove's automatic
color assignment logic.

This PR adds a new `optional` attribute to the `FoxgloveMessageField`
type and applies this to the `color` field.

For ROS, we continue requiring users to to provide those values. This is
documented in the schemas README

data.author: surbina
data.message:
notebook: set up jupyter notebook widget (#663)

### Changelog
<!-- Write a one-sentence summary of the user-impacting change (API,
UI/UX, performance, etc) that could appear in a changelog. Write "None"
if there is no user-facing change -->
Add Python notebook integrations

### Docs

<!-- Link to a Docs PR, tracking ticket in Linear, OR write "None" if no
documentation changes are needed. -->
https://github.com/foxglove/docs/pull/1022

### Description

This PR adds the notebook integration for Python, particularly:
- Adds the notebook integration using Anywidget
- Uses @foxglove/embed to show Foxglove in a notebook
- Provides an API to load a layout from a file
- Provides an API to visualize messages from buffer associated with a
Context
- Add build script and update CI jobs where necessary
- Adds API docs

The integration is shipped as an extra dependency of the foxglove-sdk
package. To install, run the following command:

```bash
pip install foxglove-sdk[notebook]
```


https://drive.google.com/file/d/1BSLsBt6WSsnnj3a6G-hWsivNr8vzNZnp/view?usp=sharing

<!--Log-view workflow:

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1n45DwcTnxdjvStrMAd_93zLc-2KFC4kI/view?usp=sharing

Log-reload workflow:

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zEcR7xSxrQ2e7ODgigkBYz-d9gYl_yT_/view?usp=sharing-->

<!--With global context:-->

<!--img width="687" height="797" alt="Screenshot 2025-09-23 at 17 38 31"
src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82401306-f635-45bb-8de5-81fc40095e79"
/-->
<!--img width="539" height="791" alt="Screenshot 2025-10-01 at 18 58 15"
src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85b39f4c-a5fe-4b13-bd65-b8b52feb328b"
/-->

/-->

src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7377d549-dad2-4a9d-a2cb-6757b22a984f"
/-->
<!--img width="540" height="850" alt="Screenshot 2025-10-02 at 17 36 11"

/-->
<!--img width="547" height="866" alt="Screenshot 2025-10-07 at 10 44 30"
src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e1ec0b5-2623-47c4-90e4-cd74f5d74cf4"
/-->

### How to test?

1. Clone this repo
1. Follow instructions
[here](https://github.com/foxglove/foxglove-sdk/blob/sebastian/ert-577-set-up-widget-package/python/foxglove-sdk/CONTRIBUTING.md)
to setup the repo for local development
1. Activate the virtual environment created by poetry
    ```bash
    eval $(poetry env activate)
    ```
1. Install Jupyter
    ```bash
    pip install jupyterlab
    ```
1. Build and install the sdk locally, including the notebook extra
dependencies
    ```bash
    pip install -e ".[notebook]"
    ```
1. Launch Jupyter
    ```bash
    jupyter lab
    ```

Example notebooks:
1. Log-view flow:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iBURbNQjiOtXc8Onk1vjhTwdd8q2VATq/view?usp=drive_link
2. Log-reload flow:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/12Ftbu-qpXIdwSJln4_Dy7FefcoJjctO1/view?usp=drive_link

<!-- If necessary, link relevant Linear or Github issues. Use `Fixes:
foxglove/repo#1234` to auto-close the Github issue or Fixes: FG-### for
Linear isses. -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Greg Smith <gasmith@foxglove.dev>

data.author: achim-k
data.message:
Do not normalize time/duration types in generateRosMsgDefinition (#115)

### Public-Facing Changes

Do not normalize `time` & `duration` types in `generateRosMsgDefinition`

### Description
Changes `generateRosMsgDefinition` to not normalize `time` & `duration`
fields to their ROS2 equivalents (e.g. `builtin_interfaces/Time`). This
is now done in `generateRosMsg` as `generateRosMsgDefinition` should
output a message definition which is compliant with
[@foxglove/message-definition](https://github.com/foxglove/message-definition)
where e.g. `builtin_interfaces/Time` does not exist.

See
https://github.com/foxglove/rosmsg2-serialization/pull/17#issuecomment-1608102161

data.author: clalancette
data.message:
Make sure to add rustfmt and clippy to the setup-rust-toolchain action. (#671)

### Changelog

None.

### Docs

None.

### Description

In recent pull requests, the CI/rust action always seemed to be failing
with `error: 'cargo-fmt' is not installed for the toolchain
'stable-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu'.`. So we make sure to always install
it.

This should make 'cargo fmt' and 'cargo clippy' always work.

Signed-off-by: Chris Lalancette <clalancette@gmail.com>

data.author: ericmlujan
data.message:
Introduce strict build and configure CI to use it (#509) ### Changelog None ### Docs None ### Description Enable a set of Clang and MSVC compiler warnings and set them to fail builds if the `STRICT` compilation flag is set. This flag is set `true` by default in containerized builds, including the ones that run as a pre-merge check in GitHub Actions. To disable these warnings, a user can run `make STRICT=false` from the `cpp/` directory. This PR also contains changes required to address the warnings in the existing SDK codebase.
BeforeAfter
Builds fail if compiler warnings are enabled and escalated to errors Builds pass with compiler warnings enabled
Fixes: FG-12163

data.author: joe-saronic
data.message:
Added schemars-chrono feature (#851)

### Changelog

A new `schemars-chrono` Cargo feature enables the `chrono04` feature on
the underlying `schemars` dependency so that we can have automatic
schema generation for types including `chrono` timestamps.

### Docs

None

### Description

Building with `schemars` feature is nice, but sometimes you need
`chrono` support too. Enabling `chrono` feature does not enable
`chorno04` on the underlying `schemars` dependency, so you miss out on
some possibilities. The new feature ensures that you can generate
schemas with `chrono` date-time types.

---------

Co-authored-by: Jeff Zellner <jeffz@eml.cc>

data.author: ndmmdn
data.message:
Add Client Count Topic to Foxglove_bridge (#692) ### Changelog - Foxglove_bridge now publishes the number of foxglove clients connected to it under `/foxglove_bridge/client_count` ### Docs - I added a mention of the topic to the foxglove_bridge [readme](https://github.com/ndmmdn/foxglove-sdk/blob/feat-client-count/ros/src/foxglove_bridge/README.md) and that it requires a parameter to be set. ### Description - There are several examples where it is useful to no if a client is connected to the foxglove bridge, for example to pause other bandwidth intensive processes. Additionally, similar to the `who` command in bash, it can also be useful to know if there are multiple people connected to the same robot. Both of these cases can now be detected via the new client count topic. there exists the [test_callbacks](https://github.com/ndmmdn/foxglove-sdk/blob/feat-client-count/cpp/test_callbacks.cpp) test to allow testing on the c++ server level. Additionally, the new functionality was tested by build and running the foxglove_bridge as described in the readme. In a separate terminal, the `/foxglove_bridge/client_count` topic was subscribed to. Then using several foxglove instances on the same laptop, connections and disconnections were made to verify the client count is updated correctly.
BeforeAfter
This is a refactor/adaptation of [this foxglove_bridge PR](https://github.com/foxglove/ros-foxglove-bridge/pull/376) --------- Co-authored-by: Eric Lujan Co-authored-by: Eric Lujan

data.author: Blackjack200
data.message:
C++: Add initial session_id support via WebSocketServerOptions (#822)

### Changelog

C++ SDK: Add optional `session_id` to WebSocketServerOptions for setting
an initial session ID when starting the server. Previously, `session_id`
could only be set after server start via `clearSession()`.

### Docs

None. This change adds an optional field that matches the existing
behavior in the Rust and Python SDKs, which already document this
parameter.

### Description

#### Problem

As reported in https://github.com/orgs/foxglove/discussions/1209, the
C++ SDK lacked the ability to set an initial `session_id` when starting
a server. This was inconsistent with the Rust and Python SDKs, which
both support setting `session_id` via server options.

#### Change

Added `session_id` field to WebSocketServerOptions in the C++ SDK,
allowing users to specify an initial session ID when creating a server:

```cpp
foxglove::WebSocketServerOptions options;
options.context = context;
options.name = "my-server";
options.session_id = "my-custom-session-id";  // Now supported!
auto server = foxglove::WebSocketServer::create(std::move(options));
```

This also required adding the `session_id` parameter to the C API
(foxglove_server_options struct) and its implementation, so the C++
layer can pass the value through to the underlying Rust SDK.

#### Testing

- Added new test case "Initial session id" in
`cpp/foxglove/tests/test_server.cpp` that verifies the session ID is
correctly set in the initial serverInfo message

Fixes: FLE-190

data.author: sanderpick
data.message:
MCAP attachments (#791)

### Changelog

Add `McapWriterHandle::attach()` method and `McapAttachment` type to
enable writing attachments to MCAP files via the Rust SDK.

### Docs

Is there a docs repo?

### Description

This PR exposes the mcap crate's attachment functionality through the
foxglove SDK, allowing users to store arbitrary binary data
(configuration files, calibration data, images, etc.) alongside messages
in MCAP recordings.

#### Changes
- Added `McapAttachment` type re-export from the mcap crate
- Added `McapSink::attach()` internal implementation
- Added `McapWriterHandle::attach()` public API with documentation and
example
- Added comprehensive tests for attachment functionality

#### Usage example
```rust
use std::borrow::Cow;
use foxglove::{McapWriter, McapAttachment};

let mcap = McapWriter::new()
    .create_new_buffered_file("test.mcap")
    .expect("create failed");

mcap.attach(&McapAttachment {
    log_time: 0,
    create_time: 0,
    name: "config.json".to_string(),
    media_type: "application/json".to_string(),
    data: Cow::Borrowed(br#"{"setting": true}"#),
}).expect("attach failed");

mcap.close().expect("close failed");
```

#### Testing
Added unit tests for basic attachment, multiple attachments, and error
handling when attaching after close. All 212 tests pass with `--features
unstable`. Manual testing works:
```
$ mcap info test.mcap 
library:   foxglove-sdk-rust/v0.0.0                                    
profile:                                                               
messages:  3204                                                        
duration:  1m22.913993797s                                             
start:     2025-12-10T13:34:08.054447689-07:00 (1765398848.054447689)  
end:       2025-12-10T13:35:30.968441486-07:00 (1765398930.968441486)  
compression:
	lz4: [732/732 chunks] [1.36 GiB/968.95 MiB (30.44%)] [11.69 MiB/sec] 
chunks:
	max uncompressed size: 1.91 MiB
	max compressed size: 1.34 MiB
	overlaps: no
channels:
	[redacted]
channels: 5
attachments: 1
metadata: 1
```

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Adds support for writing MCAP attachments via
`McapWriterHandle::attach` and re-exports `McapAttachment`, with sink
implementation and tests.
> 
> - **Public API**
> - Re-export `McapAttachment` from `mcap` in `rust/foxglove/src/lib.rs`
and `rust/foxglove/src/mcap_writer.rs`.
> - Add `McapWriterHandle::attach(&McapAttachment)` with docs and
example.
> - **MCAP Sink**
> - Implement `McapSink::attach(&mcap::Attachment)` to write
attachments.
> - **Tests**
> - Add unit tests for attachments: basic write, multiple attachments,
and error after close in `rust/foxglove/src/mcap_writer/mcap_sink.rs`
tests.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
2adb09da41a5ef5b4206143fef54661d9474f363. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

Update VoxelGrid docs to be more in line with Grid for clarity (#775) ### Changelog Update VoxelGrid docs ### Docs https://linear.app/foxglove/issue/ERT-1111/voxelgrid-positionorientation-is-wrong ### Description Update VoxelGrid docs to be in line with Grid Docs

Will read "Origin of the grid’s lower-front-left corner in the reference frame. The grid’s pose is defined relative to this corner, so an untransformed grid with an identity orientation has this corner at the origin."
Fixes: ERT-1111 --- > [!NOTE] > Clarifies `VoxelGrid.pose` to reference the grid’s lower-front-left corner and pose semantics across all schema/message definitions. > > - **Schemas and message definitions**: > - Clarify `VoxelGrid.pose` description to: origin is the grid’s lower-front-left corner; pose defined relative to this corner. > - Updated across C (`c/include/foxglove-c/foxglove-c.h`), Rust (`c/src/generated_types.rs`, `rust/foxglove/src/schemas/foxglove.rs`), C++ (`cpp/foxglove/include/foxglove/schemas.hpp`), Python docs (`python/foxglove-sdk/src/generated/schemas.rs`), ROS1/ROS2 msgs (`ros*/VoxelGrid.msg`, `schemas/ros*/VoxelGrid.msg`), Flatbuffers (`schemas/flatbuffer/VoxelGrid.fbs`), JSON Schema (`schemas/jsonschema/*.json`, `schemas/jsonschema/index.ts`), OMG IDL (`schemas/omgidl/foxglove/VoxelGrid.idl`), Protobuf (`schemas/proto/foxglove/VoxelGrid.proto`), and TypeScript types/snapshots (`typescript/schemas/src/**`). > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0bbcabab615bf6547463d55189e74fa2bda1c18e. data.author: chencaidy
data.message:
foxglove_bridge: fix message format error when using cache (#769)

### Changelog
<!-- Write a one-sentence summary of the user-impacting change (API,
UI/UX, performance, etc) that could appear in a changelog. Write "None"
if there is no user-facing change -->

Fix message format error when using cache, foxglove shows "Failed to
parse channel schema".

### Docs

<!-- Link to a Docs PR, tracking ticket in Linear, OR write "None" if no
documentation changes are needed. -->

None

### Description

<!-- Describe the problem, what has changed, and motivation behind those
changes. Pretend you are advocating for this change and the reader is
skeptical. -->

foxglove_bridge contains a hard-coded issue when retrieving cached
message files, causing IDL files to be correctly recognized only on the
first attempt. Subsequent cached requests return an incorrect format.

Here is the first discussions:
https://github.com/orgs/foxglove/discussions/1195

This fix was tested on my computer, both MSG and IDL worked well.

data.author: MatthewDHarrison
data.message:
Fix memory leak in generic_client allocate_message (#667)

### Changelog

None

### Docs

None

### Description

We have found that with a high volume of service calls the memory usage
of the foxglove bridge grows unbounded. Using valgrind with memcheck,
the source of some of these definitely lost blocks was found to be the
`allocate_message` function in `generic_client`, where a buffer has
memory allocated using the `MessageMembers` `_init_function` which is
freed using `free`. This seems to mean that if a message type contains
pointers, the memory pointed to will never be freed.

To remedy this, we instead call `members->_fini_function` , which the
Message-specific clean-up functions (as documented
[here](https://docs.ros.org/en/ros2_packages/humble/api/rosidl_typesupport_introspection_cpp/generated/structrosidl__typesupport__introspection__cpp_1_1MessageMembers__s.html)
and implemented
[here](https://github.com/ros2/rosidl/blob/4edcac1c0aa162d4c231389811c105214c74a439/rosidl_typesupport_introspection_cpp/resource/msg__type_support.cpp.em#L253)

data.author: jlack1987
data.message:
Fix template type issue (#538)

### Changelog
Fix a compiler error involving template types.

### Docs

None

### Description

When trying to compile on Ubuntu 24.04 I get the following compiler
error
```bash
foxglove-sdk/cpp/foxglove/include/foxglove/server/parameter.hpp:335:50: error: expected unqualified-id before ‘.’ token
  335 |       result.emplace(dictKey, dictValue.template .get<T>());
      |                                                  ^
foxglove-sdk/cpp/foxglove/include/foxglove/server/parameter.hpp:335:56: error: expected primary-expression before ‘>’ token
  335 |       result.emplace(dictKey, dictValue.template .get<T>());
      |                                                        ^
foxglove-sdk/cpp/foxglove/include/foxglove/server/parameter.hpp:335:58: error: expected primary-expression before ‘)’ token
  335 |       result.emplace(dictKey, dictValue.template .get<T>());
```

<!-- If necessary, link relevant Linear or Github issues. Use `Fixes:
foxglove/repo#1234` to auto-close the Github issue or Fixes: FG-### for
Linear isses. -->

Co-authored-by: Jordan Lack <jordan@terradepth.com>

paulsohn
data.message:
C++: Include missing <vector> (#506)

### Changelog

C++: Add missing `#include <vector>` to prevent build failure.

### Docs

None

### Description

I am following https://docs.foxglove.dev/docs/sdk/example step by step
with foxglove sdk v0.9.0, in Ubuntu 22.04 / Clang 14.0.0, created
`CMakeLists.txt` with the given content, set up the `main.cpp` and built
with
```bash
$ mkdir build
$ cd build
$ cmake ..
$ cmake --build .
```

The last build command fails with the following error:
```

  std::vector<foxglove_string> ids;

/usr/lib/llvm-14/bin/../include/c++/v1/iosfwd:260:28: note: template is declared here

/home/sohn/workspace/foxglove-sdk-bridge/build/_deps/foxglove-src/src/server/connection_graph.cpp:47:32: error: implicit instantiation of undefined template 'std::vector<foxglove_string>'
  std::vector<foxglove_string> ids;
                               ^
/usr/lib/llvm-14/bin/../include/c++/v1/iosfwd:260:28: note: template is declared here
class _LIBCPP_TEMPLATE_VIS vector;
                           ^
/home/sohn/workspace/foxglove-sdk-bridge/build/_deps/foxglove-src/src/server/connection_graph.cpp:48:27: error: implicit instantiation of undefined template 'std::vector<std::string>'
  ids.reserve(provider_ids.size());
                          ^

  for (const auto& id : provider_ids) {

/usr/lib/llvm-14/bin/../include/c++/v1/iosfwd:260:28: note: template is declared here
class _LIBCPP_TEMPLATE_VIS vector;

9 errors generated.
gmake[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/foxglove-sdk-bridge.dir/build.make:188: CMakeFiles/foxglove-sdk-bridge.dir/_deps/foxglove-src/src/server/connection_graph.cpp.o] Error 1
gmake[1]: *** [CMakeFiles/Makefile2:83: CMakeFiles/foxglove-sdk-bridge.dir/all] Error 2
gmake: *** [Makefile:91: all] Error 2
```

<!-- In addition to unit tests, describe any manual testing you did to
validate this change. -->

It turns out `foxglove/server/connection_graph.hpp` is using
`std::vector` but `<vector>` is not included by itself or any included
headers. I manually added this inclusion in
`build/_deps/foxglove-src/include/foxglove/server/connection_graph.hpp`
and confirmed that the build succeeds.

data.author: fgwt202412
data.message:
Fix CI errors due to ubuntu 24.04 image rollout (#160)

### Changelog
<!-- Write a one-sentence summary of the user-impacting change (API,
UI/UX, performance, etc) that could appear in a changelog. Write "None"
if there is no user-facing change -->

None

### Docs

<!-- Link to a Docs PR, tracking ticket in Linear, OR write "None" if no
documentation changes are needed. -->

None

### Description

<!-- Describe the problem, what has changed, and motivation behind those
changes. Pretend you are advocating for this change and the reader is
skeptical. -->

CI [broke
recently](https://github.com/foxglove/schemas/actions/runs/12341162294/job/34439471378?pr=159)
because Python 3.7 is not supported for Ubuntu 24.04 x64. See
https://github.com/actions/setup-python/issues/962. This fixes the
`python` job to use Ubuntu 22.04.

Fixes #68

data.author: jameskuszmaul-brt
data.message:
Add explicit field IDs to flatbuffer schemas (#148)

### Changelog
Added explicit field IDs to flatbuffer schemas.

### Docs

None

### Description

This allows the schemas to be compiled with `--require-explicit-ids`
turned on in `flatc`, which can be nice to help prevent people from
accidentally making breaking changes to flatbuffer schemas if they
choose to reorder fields (or insert new fields in the middle of the
schemas). Because of how flatc works with includes, if downstream users
want to use `--require-explicit-ids` on their own schemas that happen to
include foxglove schema types, they must update the foxglove schemas
that they use to include IDs.

data.author: foxymiles
data.message:
Add typescript schema export utility (#119)

None

### Description
Add typescript schema export utility. This will be used in studio to
import schema definitions in order to make them available for use in
user scripts.
